description: Upgrade the document parsing wiki into an evidence-first, schema-first, patch-based, harness-gated knowledge compiler.

userStories.title: Define the versioned Canonical DocIR schema
userStories.description: As a knowledge engineer, I want a versioned Canonical DocIR schema so that every parser output can normalize into one canonical contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Define top-level DocIR fields including doc_id, source_id, parser, parser_version, schema_version, pages, blocks, relations, warnings, and confidence
Define required Page fields including page_no, width, height, rotation, image_uri or equivalent, ocr_used, reading_order_version, blocks, and warnings
Define required Block fields including block_id, page_no, block_type, reading_order, bbox, parent_id, children_ids, text fields, citations, confidence, source_parser, and source_node_id
Define supported relation types including caption_of, footnote_of, continued_from, continued_to, references, mentioned_in, same_table_as, same_section_as, duplicate_of, and derived_from
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define the versioned patch and page schemas
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want explicit patch and page schemas so that wiki changes, page metadata, and review decisions are machine-checkable.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a patch schema with patch_id, run_id, source_id, generated_at, changes, blast_radius, risk_score, review_required, and merge_status
Define a page schema or template contract for source, entity, concept, failure, comparison, and decision pages
Require frontmatter fields such as id, type, title, schema_version, created_at, updated_at, source_docs, related_claims, and review_status where applicable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Externalize router, threshold, and review policy config
userStories.description: As an operator, I want routing, release thresholds, and review policies in config files so that system behavior does not depend on hidden prompt logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create external config contracts for parser routes, risk thresholds, release gates, and review policies
Support environment-specific values for local, dev, staging, and prod
Document how route selection and release decisions read from config instead of hardcoded prompt branches
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create the source registry record contract
userStories.description: As the system, I want a stable source registry record so that each uploaded document can be tracked across runs, parser versions, and wiki outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Define required source record fields including source_id, source_hash, file_name, mime_type, byte_size, created_at, ingested_at, ingest_count, origin, tags, owner, status, latest_run_id, and latest_docir_id
Ensure source_id is stable and unique for each document
Define source-to-page and source-to-review linkage fields or indexes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add immutable raw source storage
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want immutable raw source storage so that no LLM or repair step can overwrite the original evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist each original source into a read-only raw source location
Store metadata that links the immutable source copy back to the source registry record
Prevent write paths from overwriting the immutable original file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add hash-based duplicate detection and ingest history
userStories.description: As the system, I want duplicate detection and ingest history so that repeated imports reuse source identity and remain auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect duplicate documents by hash before creating a new source identity
Increment ingest history for repeated imports of the same source
Record the parser version and run metadata for each ingest attempt
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define the parser backend interface
userStories.description: As a parser engineer, I want a common parser backend interface so that multiple parser implementations can plug into the same pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Define parser backend methods for parse(file), normalize(raw_result), export_debug_assets(...), healthcheck(), and capabilities()
Document required capability metadata for route selection
Ensure the interface supports both primary and fallback parser backends
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement route decision output and reason logging
userStories.description: As a parser engineer, I want route decisions to be explicit and logged so that route selection is explainable and debuggable.
acceptanceCriteria: Route selection outputs selected_route, primary_parser, fallback_parsers, expected_risks, post_parse_repairs, and review_policy
Route logging records the document signals used to choose the route
Route selection reads from external config rather than hardcoded prompt branches
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement fallback parser execution policy
userStories.description: As the system, I want fallback parser behavior to be automatic and explicit so that parser failures do not silently degrade data quality.
acceptanceCriteria: When the primary parser fails, automatically execute a configured fallback parser
Record the failure reason, the fallback_used flag, and the selected fallback parser
Mark fallback runs for stricter review policy
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist parser raw outputs and debug assets
userStories.description: As a parser engineer, I want each run to keep its raw parser outputs and debug assets so that parsing issues can be reproduced and reviewed.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist raw parser output, parser logs, rendered page images, and debug screenshots or overlays per run
Link persisted debug assets to the source and run records
Ensure debug assets remain available after normalization and repair steps
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Normalize page-local parser output into DocIR pages and blocks
userStories.description: As the system, I want page-local parser output normalized into DocIR pages and blocks so that downstream steps receive one consistent structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Convert parser output into DocIR pages, blocks, and initial relations
Preserve reading_order, bbox, text content, and block hierarchy
Represent unknown content as unknown blocks instead of dropping it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add document-global repair for cross-page structure
userStories.description: As the system, I want document-global repair so that cross-page tables, headings, references, and repeated layout noise are normalized after page-local parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Repair heading hierarchy, repeated header or footer noise, cross-page tables, cross-page notes, and caption attachment where applicable
Preserve continuity relations for cross-page objects
Write the repaired result back as canonical DocIR without deleting the original parser output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record structured repair logs
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want every repair to have a before-and-after record so that normalization is auditable and debuggable.
acceptanceCriteria: Each repair record stores repair_id, repair_type, before, after, reason, confidence, performed_by, and timestamp
Repair logs link back to the affected source and run
Repair logs are preserved alongside the canonical DocIR result
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract entities from canonical DocIR
userStories.description: As a knowledge engineer, I want entities extracted from canonical DocIR so that the knowledge layer is built on structure instead of free-form summary text.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract entity records from DocIR using a defined entity type set
Store enough metadata to link each entity back to source evidence
Preserve candidate aliases without implicitly merging entities
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract claims and relations from canonical DocIR
userStories.description: As a knowledge engineer, I want claims and relations extracted from canonical DocIR so that the knowledge graph is explicit and maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract structured claims and relations from normalized document content
Persist claim status fields needed for supported, inferred, and conflicted claims
Link claims to source pages or blocks before wiki compilation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce evidence and inference validation rules
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want evidence and inference rules enforced so that claims cannot be presented as authoritative without traceable support.
acceptanceCriteria: Require supported claims to include at least one evidence anchor
Require inferred claims to include an inference note
Require conflicted claims to reference the conflicting source set
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compile source pages from DocIR and extracted knowledge
userStories.description: As an end user, I want a source page for each document so that I can inspect the document summary, structure, and evidence links from one entry point.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate one source page per source with required frontmatter
Include source metadata, structural summary, and links to evidence-backed claims or related entities
Generate stable wiki links for the source page output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compile entity pages incrementally
userStories.description: As an end user, I want entity pages to update incrementally so that the knowledge base evolves without full rewrites.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate new entity pages when new entities appear
Update existing entity pages through patch proposals rather than direct overwrite
Preserve aliases, related claims, and source_docs metadata on entity pages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compile concept pages incrementally
userStories.description: As an end user, I want concept pages to update incrementally so that shared concepts can absorb new evidence without losing prior context.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate concept pages from extracted claims and relations
Update concept pages through patch proposals rather than direct overwrite
Preserve frontmatter, source_docs, related_claims, and review_status metadata
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compile failure, comparison, and decision pages
userStories.description: As a team, I want failure, parser comparison, and decision pages so that hard cases and operational decisions become first-class knowledge objects.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate or update failure pages for recurring parse failures
Generate parser comparison pages that summarize fidelity, structure, and cost differences
Generate decision pages or equivalent records for important review outcomes or operating decisions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate structured wiki patch artifacts
userStories.description: As the system, I want wiki changes emitted as structured patches so that changes can be linted, reviewed, approved, and rolled back safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Emit create_page and update_page operations in a structured patch artifact
Record the patch target pages and change summary for each generated patch
Do not write final wiki output directly without a patch artifact
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compute patch risk score and blast radius
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want each patch scored for risk and blast radius so that large or risky changes automatically receive more scrutiny.
acceptanceCriteria: Compute a risk score for each patch
Compute blast radius metrics including affected pages, claims, and links
Mark high-risk or high-blast-radius patches as review_required
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run structural and knowledge lint before merge
userStories.description: As the system, I want structural and knowledge lint before merge so that invalid schema, broken links, and unsupported claims are blocked early.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect broken links, orphan pages, invalid ids, duplicate ids, missing frontmatter, and invalid page types
Detect supported claims without evidence, inferred claims without inference notes, conflicted claims without conflict sources, and duplicate entity candidates
Classify lint findings into P0, P1, P2, and P3 severities
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Block auto-merge on release gate failures
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want release gates enforced automatically so that invalid or regressed outputs do not merge into the formal wiki.
acceptanceCriteria: Prevent auto-merge when P0 lint exists
Prevent auto-merge when harness has not run or when regression exceeds thresholds
Prevent auto-merge when unsupported claims increase or when fallback output is below confidence policy
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run harness and publish ingest reports
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want a harness report for every ingest run so that parse quality, knowledge quality, and maintenance quality are measurable over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Run harness for every ingest run
Publish an ingest report with parse quality, knowledge quality, and maintenance quality sections
Persist the harness output in a queryable report location
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish regression comparison reports
userStories.description: As a parser engineer, I want regression comparison reports across re-ingest runs so that parser and schema changes can be evaluated instead of guessed.
acceptanceCriteria: Compare current ingest output against a previous run or golden baseline
Publish a regression report that highlights changed claims, links, and structural outputs
Make the regression result available to release gate logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the review queue backend
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a review queue backend so that high-risk patches, conflicted claims, and complex parsing cases can be triaged in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Store review queue entries for high-risk patches and review-required objects
Track reviewer, timestamp, target object, decision, reason, linked patch, and optional note
Support approve, reject, and request-changes state transitions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the review console page shell
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a review console page shell so that I can inspect review-required documents through a dedicated UI instead of plain markdown alone.
acceptanceCriteria: Render a review console page that can open a review-required source or patch
Display the original page image or equivalent source rendering in the console
Show the associated patch or review target summary alongside the source view
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser

userStories.title: Add bbox and reading-order overlays to the review console
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want bbox and reading-order overlays in the review console so that layout and sequence problems are visible on the original page image.
acceptanceCriteria: Display block bounding boxes over the source rendering
Display reading-order overlays or numbering for the selected page
Allow the reviewer to inspect the block linked to the overlay
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser

userStories.title: Add claim drilldown and parser compare to the review console
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want claim-to-evidence drilldown and parser comparison in the review console so that I can validate knowledge outputs against source evidence and compare parser behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Allow a reviewer to open a claim and navigate to its evidence anchors
Display parser comparison data for a source in the review console
Keep the review target, source view, and evidence drilldown synchronized
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser

userStories.title: Add conflict, dedup, and deprecation workflows
userStories.description: As a knowledge maintainer, I want explicit conflict, entity dedup, and deprecation workflows so that the wiki can evolve without silently overwriting history.
acceptanceCriteria: Mark conflicted claims and preserve both sides of the evidence
Support candidate-based entity dedup suggestions without implicit merge
Support deprecated status with preserved history and replacement links where applicable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add CLI commands for the core pipeline
userStories.description: As an operator, I want CLI commands for the core pipeline so that ingest, parsing, compilation, review, and reporting can run in a repeatable local workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide CLI commands for ingest, route, parse, normalize, extract, compile, lint, eval, review list, review approve, review reject, and report
Ensure each CLI command maps to the underlying pipeline stage or review action
Document the expected inputs and outputs for each command
Typecheck passes
